behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is a deterministic slicing/ordering bug at one local preprocessing boundary. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: correctness does not depend on model output. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text is pure transform logic: "trimmed to the most recent 10 messages"; a single function test can reproduce off-by-one/order failures.
behaviors.test_description: Test the history-trimming function with 11+ messages and assert exactly the latest 10 (correct order, no older entries) are returned for the LLM payload.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this is output-quality correctness (relevance/on-topic), which depends on real model judgment and can vary run-to-run. A stub would always pass and hide the real failure. MULTI-STEP: not a required multi-agent pipeline check; it is one generation-quality decision. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text only requires one generated response quality check, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run prompts against the real LLM using a labeled relevance set (question + expected topical anchors) and assert responses are on-topic/non-generic by rubric or grader model thresholds across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: "correctly identifies ... too formal" is model-judgment quality, so mocked tone checks can hide real failures. Integration alone is insufficient for correctness of the tone decision. MULTI-STEP: this behavior hinges on one model decision (tone flag) governing handoff to regeneration, so Agentic fits; Workflow not strictly required. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration due behavior text requiring correct tone identification; rejected Workflow because it does not require validating multiple sequential model decisions beyond this gate.
behaviors.test_description: Using real model calls, submit casual-tier scenarios likely to induce formal drafts; assert the tone-check flags formality and the system issues a regeneration call/event.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module handoff (chat flow -> persistence schema/transaction), not just single-function logic. REAL-INFRA: requires DB interaction, but can be caught with local/in-process DB wiring (no mandatory real external network/OS primitive), so Integration over System. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model output quality; deterministic stubs are fine. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is persistence correctness and timestamp recording, reproducible with local DB integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run the chat service with a local test DB, force a regeneration path, then assert stored records include user message, first draft, regenerated draft, and monotonically correct timestamps/links.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is orchestration/state handoff across modules (tone-check outcome, regeneration, outward response), so higher than Unit. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess; (b) correctness is about replacement behavior, not model quality; (c) no need to validate multi-agent quality chain. Therefore Integration is minimum. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text focuses on visibility control ("customer never sees the rejected draft"), which can be validated with deterministic stubs.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service components with stubbed LLM outputs (draft1, draft2) and forced tone-trigger=true; assert returned customer payload is draft2 only, while draft1 is internal-only (e.g., audit/log) and never emitted to client channel.
